time_window.note: Start date is set when Phase 1 begins. End date auto-calculated as start + 90 days.

responsibilities: Track and report all costs transparently
Deliver revenue target within time window

parties.note: Stakeholder identities to be populated when onboarded. Domains define the evaluation lens.

tool.name: Afloat
tool.description: A no-fluff, grounded, transparent quick-chat AI assistant (~2 min max per session) focused on cognitive and decision-support tasks. Helps users get past context gates — moments where a brief summary or decision nudge unblocks engagement.

examples: meeting_triage: should I set this meeting or skip it?
priority_decisions: which of these should I tackle first?
quick_briefings: give me the gist so I can engage
context_gate_resolution: I'm stuck because I don't understand the situation
out_of_scope: Autonomously completing tasks for the user
Tasks requiring high effort or specialized credentials
Anything that replaces the user's decision-making

session_flow.name: user_input
session_flow.description: User describes their context or question

session_flow.name: gate_detection
session_flow.description: LLM identifies the context gate type (meeting_triage, priority_decision, quick_briefing, or context_gate_resolution)

session_flow.name: brief_delivery
session_flow.description: LLM returns a proportional brief (max 150 words) to unblock the user

session_flow.name: user_decision
session_flow.description: User proceeds or asks one follow-up (max 1 follow-up before session ends)

principles.name: functional
principles.definition: The tool completes its core workflow reliably without errors

principles.name: efficient
principles.definition: Responses are fast and sessions stay within the 2-minute scope

principles.name: productive
principles.definition: Users measurably get past context gates after using the tool

documentation.scope: Baseline system design, component map, session lifecycle, LLM prompt strategy, data layer schema, instrumentation hooks, API route map, security boundaries, and 9-step plain-English build sequence.

functional.description: Percentage of sessions that complete without errors

efficient.description: Time from user input to tool response per turn

productive.description: Percentage of sessions where user proceeds past the context gate

session_scope.description: Average time a user spends in a single session

revenue_net.description: Total revenue minus all costs after 90 days

transparency.description: Gross revenue covers net target plus all costs with zero hidden extras

session_success_rate.collection: Log session_completed boolean per session at session end

response_latency.collection: Timestamp diff between request_received and response_sent per turn

context_gate_pass_rate.collection: Log user_proceeded boolean per session (true if user engages after brief)

avg_session_duration.collection: Timestamp diff between session_start and session_end

selected_model_details.name: Per-Session Metered
selected_model_details.rationale: Usage-based pricing aligned with 91% SaaS market trend (Maxio 2025). Base subscription provides predictable revenue floor. Metered overage captures power user value. Parameters tunable via Redis-backed param-store without redeployment.

llm_api.basis: ~2 min sessions, small token footprint, 90-day projection

hosting.basis: Vercel/Netlify/Railway free tier or local-first deployment

marketing.basis: Social media, word of mouth, optional $5 ad spend

domain.basis: Free subdomain available; $1 if custom domain desired

redis.basis: Free tier (10K commands/day). Monitor for overages at $0.20/100K commands.

revenue.surplus_note: Margin is tight (-$0.24). Quarterly billing reduces Stripe fee drag vs monthly ($2.24 vs $4.64). Any acquisition above 12 subscriber-quarters covers the shortfall.
revenue.rule: gross_revenue >= net_target + sum(all_costs); zero hidden extras. If actual costs exceed estimates, gross target adjusts upward to preserve $22 net.

acquisition_stress_test.billing_note: Quarterly billing at $9/qtr. Stripe fee per transaction: $0.30 + 2.9% = $0.561. Users subscribe once per quarter.

expected_case.note: Misses $22 target by $0.24. 1 additional subscriber covers the gap.

worst_case.mitigation: Activate Model C (one-time $5 purchase) as supplementary channel by day 60

ledger.description: Real-time transparency ledger. Every financial event is recorded with itemized breakdown.

entry.category: subscription | llm_api | hosting | marketing | domain | payment_processing | redis | other

entries.description: Ledger initialization — test entry to validate audit trail

items.name: Define cognitive scope boundaries
items.description: Defined in tool.scope: meeting triage, priority decisions, quick briefings, context gate resolution.

items.name: Design session flow
items.description: 4-step flow defined in tool.scope.session_flow: user_input → gate_detection → brief_delivery → user_decision.

items.name: System architecture
items.description: Defined in tool.architecture: LLM layer (gpt-4o-mini), session controller (3 turns / 120s), JSON data layer, Stripe payment, minimal chat UI on Vercel.

items.name: Baseline instrumentation plan
items.description: Defined in baselines.instrumentation: collection method, storage, and reporting cadence for all four operational metrics.

items.name: Build core tool
items.description: Implement the cognitive-assistive chat with session scope enforcement. Session controller, LLM layer, chat UI, and 4-step flow all implemented.

items.name: Agent-based reliability testing
items.description: Automated testing for session success rate, latency, and scope compliance. 118+ tests + reliability-run.ts.

items.name: Session scope validation
items.description: Verify sessions stay within ~2 min and tool provides proportional assistance. 2-min + 2-turn enforcement tested.

items.name: Payment/revenue integration
items.description: Stripe checkout, webhooks, and idempotency implemented.

items.name: Update contract.json v1.1
items.description: Contract updated through v1.5.0 with actual build data.

items.name: Soft launch
items.description: Code ready, deployment pending real-world configuration (Stripe, Vercel env vars).

items.name: User acquisition
items.description: Organic acquisition targeting ~1 new subscriber per week.

items.name: Daily revenue vs. ledger tracking
items.description: Record all revenue and costs in the transparency ledger daily.

items.name: Final contract.json v1.2
items.description: Contract with actuals vs. projections, final ledger.

items.name: Stakeholder reliability report
items.description: Summary for Art, Market, and Finance domain stakeholders.

items.name: Financial verification
items.description: Confirm net >= $22, all costs covered, zero hidden extras.

compliance.transparency: All costs declared upfront in estimated_costs and tracked in real-time via the revenue ledger. No cost category may be added post-hoc without updating gross_target to preserve net_target.
compliance.cost_coverage_guarantee: Gross revenue always covers net target + all actual costs. If costs exceed estimates, gross target adjusts upward automatically.
compliance.amendment_policy: Any changes to baselines, revenue targets, or compliance terms require a new contract version (version field increment) with changelog.

pre_launch_blockers: Pass all compliance test cases (TC-01 through TC-08)
pre_launch_completed: Sign DPAs with all third-party processors — auto-incorporated (verified 2026-02-26)
Draft and publish privacy policy v1.0 — published at /privacy (effective 2026-03-01)
Complete Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) — DPIA.md complete
Implement consent management UI (CM-01, CM-02, CM-03) — consent page + settings page + consent.ts
Implement data rights API endpoints (DR-01 through DR-04) — data-export, data, profile routes
Implement audit logging infrastructure — audit.ts with append-only rpush

acquisition_shortfall.action: Activate Model C (one-time $5 purchase) as supplementary channel

latency_breach.action: Downgrade prompt complexity or switch to lighter model variant

cost_overrun.action: Freeze discretionary spend (marketing, domain), raise gross target, log amendment in changelog

risk_controls.contract_amendment_rule: All changes require version increment + changelog entry + stakeholder notification within 48 hours

applicable_frameworks: DPDPA (Digital Personal Data Protection Act — regional consideration)

data_flow_audit.description: Map of all user data collection, processing, and storage points in the system.

flows.name: session_input
flows.note: User text may contain incidental PII. No PII is intentionally requested.

flows.name: llm_api_relay
flows.retention: transient (not stored by provider beyond API processing)
flows.note: OpenAI data usage policy applies. API data not used for training when using API (per OpenAI policy as of 2026).

flows.name: session_telemetry
flows.note: Telemetry contains no user text content. Session IDs are pseudonymous.

flows.name: payment_processing
flows.note: PII handled entirely by Stripe. No card data touches our servers. Stripe is PCI-DSS compliant.

flows.name: subscription_reference
flows.purpose: Link subscription state to user account for access control
flows.note: Only Stripe reference IDs stored. No email or card data stored locally.

consent_management.description: Mechanisms for obtaining, recording, and managing user consent for data processing.

requirements.name: initial_consent
requirements.mechanism: Explicit opt-in checkbox (unchecked by default) with plain-language summary
requirements.storage: consent_record in data_layer with timestamp and version

requirements.name: granular_opt_out
requirements.mechanism: Toggle switches per consent category. Changes effective immediately.

requirements.name: consent_renewal
requirements.mechanism: Re-consent prompt on next login after policy change. Service degraded (not blocked) until re-consent.

data_rights_api.description: API endpoints enabling users to exercise their data rights. All requests authenticated and logged.

endpoints.description: Returns all data associated with the authenticated user in JSON format
Permanently deletes all user data and revokes subscription. Irreversible after 7-day grace period.
endpoints.stripe_action: Cancel subscription and request Stripe customer deletion

endpoints.description: Returns user data in a portable, machine-readable format (JSON + CSV bundle)
Allows user to correct inaccurate personal data (limited to profile fields, not session logs)

data_rights_api.authentication: Bearer token (JWT) tied to authenticated session
data_rights_api.abuse_protection: Repeated deletion requests within grace period do not reset the timer

audit_logging.description: Immutable log of all data operations for compliance verification and incident response.

log_schema.action: create | read | update | delete | export | consent_change
log_schema.ip_hash: SHA-256 of client IP (not raw IP)

audit_logging.immutability: Append-only log. No edits or deletions permitted. Archived to cold storage after 90 days.
audit_logging.access_control: Read-only for auditors. Write-only for system. No admin delete capability.

alerting.bulk_deletion_request: Alert if > 5 deletion requests in 24 hours
alerting.unauthorized_access_attempt: Alert on any failed authentication to data rights endpoints
alerting.consent_revocation_spike: Alert if > 10% of users revoke consent in 7-day window

data_retention.description: Policies governing how long each data category is stored before automatic deletion or anonymization.

policies.post_retention_action: Aggregate into anonymized statistics, then delete raw logs
Discarded after LLM response is delivered. Never written to data_layer.
Delete. Stripe-side data governed by Stripe retention policy.
Archive to cold storage for 2 additional years, then delete

auto_deletion.mechanism: Scheduled job runs daily at 02:00 UTC. Scans for records past retention period.
auto_deletion.failure_handling: Retry 3x over 24 hours. Alert admin if still failing.

privacy_policy.description: Privacy policy integration requirements and versioning.
requirements: Plain-language privacy policy accessible from every page (footer link)
Policy must enumerate all data flows from data_flow_audit
Policy must explain each consent category and how to opt out
Policy must describe data rights and how to exercise them
Policy must state retention periods per data category
Policy must identify third-party processors (OpenAI, Stripe) with links to their privacy policies
Policy must include contact method for privacy inquiries

versioning.change_notification: Email + in-app banner 14 days before material changes take effect

third_party_processors.name: OpenAI
third_party_processors.dpa_mechanism: Incorporated by reference into OpenAI Services Agreement per https://openai.com/policies/data-processing-addendum/ (effective 2026-01-01)

third_party_processors.name: Stripe
third_party_processors.dpa_mechanism: Forms part of Stripe Services Agreement (SSA) per https://stripe.com/legal/dpa

third_party_processors.name: Vercel
third_party_processors.dpa_mechanism: Data Processing Addendum incorporated into Agreement per https://vercel.com/legal/dpa

testing_and_documentation.description: Validation procedures and documentation requirements for compliance verification.

test_plan.name: consent_flow_validation
test_plan.description: Verify initial consent form renders correctly, defaults to unchecked, and records consent with timestamp

test_plan.name: opt_out_effectiveness
test_plan.description: Verify that opting out of session_telemetry stops telemetry collection within same session

test_plan.name: data_export_completeness
test_plan.description: Verify GET /api/v1/user/data-export returns all user-associated data and no other user's data

test_plan.name: data_deletion_cascade
test_plan.description: Verify DELETE /api/v1/user/data removes all user data from data_layer and triggers Stripe customer deletion

test_plan.name: deletion_grace_period
test_plan.description: Verify data is recoverable within 7-day grace period and permanently gone after

test_plan.name: audit_log_immutability
test_plan.description: Verify audit logs cannot be modified or deleted via any API endpoint or direct DB access

test_plan.name: retention_auto_deletion
test_plan.description: Verify scheduled job correctly identifies and deletes expired records, and logs each deletion

test_plan.name: pii_leak_scan
test_plan.description: Verify user_text_input is never written to data_layer session logs or telemetry

documentation_requirements: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) — required before launch
Record of Processing Activities (ROPA) — derived from data_flow_audit
Incident Response Plan — procedure for data breach notification within 72 hours
Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) — signed with OpenAI, Stripe, Vercel before launch
Internal compliance runbook — step-by-step procedures for handling data rights requests

changes: Phase 2 market strategy: replaced placeholder launch-campaign pricing with real metered per-session billing model
Billing model changed from flat subscription + per-minute metered to per-session metered with base subscription
Tiers reduced from 6 (free_trial, starter, pro, session_pack, access_pass, legacy aliases) to 3 (free_trial, starter, pro)
Removed session_pack and access_pass tiers — deferred until real usage data establishes baseline patterns
Removed legacy trial/continuous aliases from TIER_LIMITS
Starter tier: 4 LLM calls, 5-minute sessions, 10 sessions/day cap (server-enforced), 200 included sessions/month
Pro tier: 8 LLM calls, 30-minute sessions, unlimited daily, 1000 included sessions/month, full model catalog + deep_read
Free trial: 5 total sessions (server-enforced, was dead code at 3), small model catalog only
Per-session overage price: $0.10/session, reported to Stripe Billing Meter (event: afloat_sessions)
Created pricing-config.ts as unified source of truth for all tier parameters
Created param-store.ts for Redis-backed live parameter adjustment without redeployment
Created analytics-triggers.ts with 6 event-driven triggers (churn, ceiling, quality, latency, conversion, cost)
Added 4 new API routes: subscription/status, trial/status, analytics/lifecycle, admin/params
Added 3 analytics cron routes: daily (03:00 UTC), weekly (Sunday 04:00 UTC), monthly (1st 05:00 UTC)
Wired emitEvent() into webhook handler for subscription_started, subscription_upgraded, subscription_canceled
Wired incrementTrialSession() into session start route for free_trial enforcement
Wired incrementDailySessionCount() into session start route for daily cap enforcement
Session metered usage reported at session start (replaced legacy per-minute reporting at session end)
Subscribe route now uses createTierCheckout with tier+billing metadata (replaced legacy trial/continuous switch)
Updated contract.json architecture, payment_layer, and revenue model to reflect Phase 2 metered model
Phase 1 (90-day launch window 2026-03-01 to 2026-05-29) archived as complete
Model inventory corrected: primary LLM is Ollama-first (not gpt-4o-mini), lifeguard is gpt-5.4
Added tier system: trial ($9/qtr) and continuous ($3/hr metered) subscription tiers
Added TierLimits type system with configurable maxLlmCalls and maxDurationMs per tier
Trial tier: 2 LLM calls, 120s max. Continuous tier: 6 LLM calls, 1800s (30 min) max
Updated session controller for tier-aware enforcement (createSession, enforceSessionLimits, updateSession, getSession)
Added metered billing support: createMeteredCheckoutSession, reportUsage in stripe.ts
Subscribe route now accepts { tier } in POST body, routes to correct Stripe price
Webhook detects tier from checkout line items, stores subscription_tier and stripe_subscription_item_id on user
Session end route reports metered usage minutes for continuous tier
Redesigned subscribe page with two-tier comparison (trial left, continuous right)
Fixed pricing display mismatch: '$3/month' updated to 'from $9/quarter' across 3 files
Chat page now uses dynamic max_duration_ms from session start response, displays tier indicator
Added safety gradient layer (src/lib/safety.ts): evaluateSafetyGradient with fail-closed defaults
Safety gradient integrated into message route — blocks rapid-fire messages in continuous tier
Added STRIPE_CONTINUOUS_PRICE_ID to .env.example
Updated session_controller in architecture to document tier-aware limits
Updated payment_layer.plan to support both trial and continuous plans
Updated selected_model_details with continuous_tier pricing
